title: Yoisho Mace
body:
"**Yoisho**" (よいしょ) is a common interjection in Japan used to express effort or self-encouragement when doing something physically taxing. It is pronounced as “**heave-ho.**"

The sounds used in this resource pack is from an older TikTok meme featuring a Japanese man and woman making mochi using a kine (a traditional wooden mallet). While making mochi, they loudly shout to each other, “**Yoisho!**” and “**Hai, hai, hai!**”

This **resource pack** replaces the **Mace** sound effects with audio from that meme.

I originally thought of this idea while practicing my macing and randomly saying “_yoisho._” The thought of hearing it every time I swung the mace seemed hilarious and obnoxious, so I turned it into a **resource pack.** And that’s how this ended up existing.

**yes im that petty i wrote all of this after it got flagged**
